zones.name: Tests Gotobedtests
insights: High cohesion (1) — files are tightly interconnected
4 circular dependency chains detected — see imports.json for details
Hub: Sources/GoToBedCore/Models/Schedule.swift is imported by 3 zones — cross-cutting dependency
Hub: Sources/GoToBedCore/Models/Validation.swift is imported by 3 zones — cross-cutting dependency
Hub: Sources/GoToBedCore/Store/Store.swift is imported by 3 zones — cross-cutting dependency
Bidirectional coupling: "log-overlaycontroller" ↔ "sources" (7+8 crossings) — consider extracting shared interface
Size imbalance: largest zone has 24 files vs smallest with 3 — uneven decomposition
4 circular dependency chains detected — see imports.json for details

App-core and app-orchestration form a two-tier architecture: app-core provides isolated domain and UI logic; app-orchestration coordinates lifecycle. The asymmetric coupling (app-core has low coupling, app-orchestration has high coupling) is structurally appropriate for a composition root.

Test zones heavily favor app-core (27 + 10 = 37 total imports) over app-orchestration (4 imports), suggesting app-core domain logic is well-tested while orchestration behavior may benefit from integration test coverage.

Circular imports between app-core and app-orchestration (8 ↔ 7) indicate tight orchestration coupling; confirm this is a documented initialization seam (OverlayController and SchedulerEngine wiring) rather than runtime data-flow coupling.
File "Tests/GoToBedCoreTests/SubmessageTests.swift" has 100% of import neighbors in Sources/GoToBedCore/Models/ but lives in Tests/GoToBedCoreTests/ — consider relocating

The apparent bidirectional coupling between 'log-overlaycontroller' and 'sources' (7+8 crossings) resolves cleanly on inspection: it is a one-directional initialization seam (AppEnvironment → OverlayController/SchedulerEngine) combined with downward domain imports (OverlayController/SchedulerEngine → GoToBedCore). No runtime data flows in a cycle.

Callback injection (SchedulerEngine.onFire wired in AppEnvironment) is the architectural mechanism preventing SchedulerEngine from importing OverlayController. This pattern is load-bearing: removing it in favor of a direct import would create a genuine circular dependency between the orchestration files.

The three hub files (Schedule.swift, Validation.swift, Store.swift) are all imported exclusively downward: GoToBedCore → consumed by GoToBed. Their hub status reflects ubiquitous domain-model use, not architectural coupling concern.

The four circular dependency chains reported in imports.json are likely zone-level artifacts from community detection misclassification (Log.swift grouped with orchestration files) rather than true Swift module-level circular imports. Swift would reject genuine circular module dependencies at build time — the presence of a working build indicates these are intra-target import cycles between files, not modules.

The entire cross-zone dependency surface is funneled through a single composition root (AppEnvironment). This is an intentional hub-and-spoke pattern appropriate for a small app, but it means AppEnvironment has no substitute or abstraction layer — it is a single point of coupling failure for the entire orchestration tier.
